title: POST /api/vouches — anonymous vouch submission, IP- and email-rate-limited
problem: Open submission endpoint paired with /vouch. Visitor types vouch + email; this endpoint inserts a row with status='pending' for Marc's moderation queue. No auth required (requiring a magic link first would kill the funnel for the people most likely to vouch — past clients, peers — who don't have accounts here). Email is captured for moderator verification but never appears in the public projection. Per-email + per-IP rate limits keep the moderation queue from being flooded; tight URL validation (http/https only) prevents javascript:/data: vectors.
analysis: POST { name, email, relationship, body, link?, sessionId?, displayPref? }. CSRF-exempt (no cookie context for anon submitter; see _middleware.ts CSRF_EXEMPT_PATHS). Validation: VOUCH_LIMITS thresholds for body/name/link length, isPlausibleEmail for email format, relationship must be in VOUCH_RELATIONSHIPS enum, link if present must start with http:// or https:// (javascript:/data: rejected). If sessionId supplied, validate it matches an active session (typo-rejection — Marc shouldn't see ghost attributions). Rate limits: 3/hour per email, 5/hour per IP — both lower than session-creation rates because a vouch carries less value to the submitter and is a tempting spam vector. INSERT with status='pending'; moderators see it at /admin/vouches; approved rows surface at /vouches via the projection-stripped public endpoint.

decisions.decision: Open submission (no auth), CSRF-exempt — same rationale as /api/auth/request-link
decisions.rationale: The audience most likely to vouch (past clients, peers, mentors) doesn't have portal accounts. Requiring sign-in would filter them out. CSRF is for cookie-context attacks; anon visitor has no cookie. Per-IP + per-email rate limits + manual moderation are the anti-spam layer.
decisions.recommendation: If automated abuse ever overwhelms the rate limit, add hCaptcha (privacy-friendlier than reCAPTCHA, documentable in /pia). Don't move to authentication.

decisions.decision: Tighter rate limits than session-creation (3/h per email, 5/h per IP)
decisions.rationale: A session is high-value to the submitter (they want help); a vouch is low-value to the submitter and high-value to a spammer (free message in Marc's queue). Tighter limits filter against the typical spam pattern (one IP, many fake emails, low per-email volume) and against the targeted-email-flood pattern.
decisions.recommendation: If legitimate volume hits the limit, tune up — but track WHO hit the limit; legitimate users tend to be one or two, spammers tend to be many.

decisions.decision: URL field requires http(s); javascript:/data: rejected
decisions.rationale: The link is rendered on /vouches as a real <a href> for the visitor to click. javascript:URL or data:URL would let a hostile submitter inject script when an unsuspecting visitor clicked. The protocol check at submission rejects these vectors before they reach the DB. The same protection also stops file:// URLs which could be confusing.
decisions.recommendation: Keep the strict protocol check. If a future vouch wants to link to a mailto: target, evaluate explicitly; don't relax the rule by default.

decisions.decision: If sessionId supplied, must match a live session
decisions.rationale: Some vouches reference a specific engagement ('Marc shipped X for me'). The submission form lets them attach a sessionId for the link. If the id is a typo, the vouch attributes to a non-existent session — confusing in /admin/vouches review and impossible to link from the engagement page. Validating at submission catches the typo class of mistakes.
decisions.recommendation: If a vouch needs to reference a session that's been soft-deleted, the right answer is moderator-side resolution, not relaxing this check.

successCriteria:
- An anon visitor can submit a vouch with no auth or CSRF
- VOUCH_LIMITS thresholds enforce body/name/link length
- Email format check rejects malformed addresses
- Relationship enum check rejects unknown values
- Link protocol check rejects javascript:/data:/file:
- sessionId if present matches a live session, else 400
- Per-email and per-IP rate limits cap abuse; over-limit → 429
- Insert lands with status='pending'; moderator review required for public surfacing
implementation: functions/api/vouches.ts — onRequestPost. Validation chain via VOUCH_LIMITS + isPlausibleEmail + relationship enum + URL protocol check. Rate limit checks for both axes. INSERT with id = randomTokenB64url and status='pending'. Returns 200 { ok: true } — vouch UI doesn't need to know the row id (moderator review handles followups). Tests: functions/_lib/vouches.test.ts (limits, projection) + integration in functions/api/vouches.test.ts.
userGuide: Client → POST /api/vouches with { name, email, relationship, body, link?, sessionId?, displayPref? }. Response → 200 { ok: true }. Submitted vouch enters /admin/vouches queue; Marc approves to surface on /vouches. Field defaults: displayPref = 'first-name' (matches the public projection). To moderate as admin: /admin/vouches lists pending; approve/reject actions live there. To trigger rate-limit explicitly for testing: submit 4 vouches from the same email within an hour — the fourth gets 429.
knownLimitations: No CAPTCHA — the rate limit + manual moderation is the anti-spam layer. If automated abuse leaks through, hCaptcha is the next escalation
Email isn't verified (no double opt-in) — a determined spammer could use throwaway emails. Moderation catches output
VOUCH_LIMITS duplicates client + server validation; if updated in one place the other could drift. Server is the source of truth
Rate limit IP is from Cloudflare's forwarded headers; misconfigured proxy upstream could mis-attribute requests. Production deploy is direct CF Pages, so theoretical
URL protocol check is exact-prefix; a future requirement for relative URLs would need a relaxation. None today

statusHistory.reason: Endpoint shipped with all validation + rate limits + moderation pipeline.
Code-first doc co-located. Open submission + tight rate limits + URL protocol check all stable.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial author — P2 critical-API sweep, co-located doc for /api/vouches.
